howto_rules: Inherit from CaseOracleXxxBase classes for each phase
Use find_repo_root pattern to gracefully handle missing workspace
Return RepoRootLocatedCheck when repo root is not found
Keep checks idempotent, non-interactive, and time-bounded
All subprocess calls must have explicit timeout
Use DependencyVersionCheck for tool version verification
example_patterns: egwalker verify/command build mode via env var
egwalker custom dataclass checks for domain-specific validation

borrowed_structure.common.py: find_repo_root, RepoRootLocatedCheck, load helpers from egwalker
borrowed_structure.env_setup.py: DependencyVersionCheck for docker, FilesystemPathCheck for repo root
borrowed_structure.artifact_build.py: FilesystemPathCheck for infrastructure files and result outputs
borrowed_structure.experiment_runs.py: Custom check dataclasses for results parsing, similar to egwalker TimingsJSONSimilarityCheck

artifact_specific_adaptations: Docker-based workflow: check Docker installation instead of language toolchains
Results are produced inside Docker container and may be copied to workspace
Custom parsing for Golf results file format (tab-separated aggregated report)
Custom validation for results-perf.csv (CSV with specific columns)
Search multiple locations for results files since agent may copy them to different paths
Threshold-based checks (detection rate > 90%) instead of correlation-based
build_steps: Dockerfile must exist in repo root
run.sh must exist in repo root
Results files (results, results-perf.csv, results.tex) produced after ./run.sh
experiment_outputs: results: microbenchmark coverage report with aggregated detection rate
plan_summary: Golf artifact uses Docker to build baseline and Golf Go runtimes and run microbenchmarks. Oracle checks Docker availability, repo infrastructure, test suite presence, and validates experiment outputs (detection rate > 90%, valid CSV, TeX file exists).

step_coverage.env-001: covered_optional: hardware requirements noted but not enforceable programmatically
step_coverage.env-003: not_covered: Apple Silicon Colima setup is platform-specific, not applicable to Linux oracle
step_coverage.build-001: covered: check results files exist as evidence of successful run.sh execution
step_coverage.build-002: covered_indirect: baseline Go binary built inside Docker, verified by results existence
step_coverage.build-003: covered_indirect: Golf Go binary built inside Docker, verified by results existence
step_coverage.build-004: covered_indirect: testing harness built inside Docker, verified by results existence
step_coverage.prep-001: covered: FilesystemPathCheck for tester/tests/deadlock/ and tester/tests/correct/
step_coverage.prep-002: not_checked: BASELINE env var is set inside Docker container only
step_coverage.prep-003: not_checked: GOLF env var is set inside Docker container only
step_coverage.run-001: covered: parse results file, check structure and aggregated detection rate
step_coverage.run-002: covered: parse results-perf.csv, validate CSV structure and column presence
step_coverage.run-004: covered: aggregated detection rate > 90% parsed from results file
step_coverage.run-005: covered_partial: CSV values validated for reasonable ranges

step_gaps: env-003: Colima/Apple Silicon setup cannot be verified on Linux hosts
prep-002: $BASELINE env var only exists inside Docker container, not on host
prep-003: $GOLF env var only exists inside Docker container, not on host
build-002/003/004: individual binary builds happen inside Docker; only verifiable indirectly via results
step_augmentations: Added Dockerfile existence check (not explicitly in plan but required for Docker-based workflow)
Added search across multiple workspace locations for results files
Added CSV column structure validation for results-perf.csv
Added check that results file contains no cgo-examples entries (plan run-001 requirement)
Added check that total go instructions count is 121 (plan run-001 requirement)
